subject: Accounting
title: The General Ledger
description: A short lesson to help you apply The General Ledger.

hook.text: The journal shows what happened and when. The ledger shows each account's running total.

buildup.text: The general ledger contains every account in the chart of accounts with all its transactions.

discovery.text: Each account page shows debits, credits, and a running balance—organized by account, not by date.

twist.text: The ledger is the foundation of financial statements—balance sheets and income statements are built from it.

climax.text: Modern software calls it a database, but the concept is identical to ledgers used centuries ago.

punchline.text: The ledger is where individual entries become a financial picture.

quiz.question: How is the general ledger organized?
